name: Risotto with Shrimp Tomatoes & Sun-Dried Toma
recipeIngredient: 1/2 medium Onion, peeled and finely chopped
1 medium Clove garlic, peeled and minced
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 medium Clove garlic, peeled and minced
2/3 pound Medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare the risotto: Bring a pan of water to boil. Add the tomatoes and blanch for 1 minute. Drain and rinse briefly with cold water. Peel, seed, and coarsely chop.
2. Heat the broth-water combination in a saucepan over low heat. Keep warm.
3. In a large skillet or pot, preferably nonstick, heat the butter and olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic; sauté for 5 minutes. Add the tomatoes and cook until softened and starting to break down, about 5 minutes. Stir in the rice and cook for 2 minutes.
4. Slowly start to add the liquid, about a ladleful at a time. Cook over low heat, stirring frequently. Wait until the liquid has been absorbed each time before adding the next ladleful. The risotto should cook in about 35 minutes. Stir in the sun-dried tomato pesto during the last 10 minutes of cooking time. Stir in the salt and pepper.
5. When the risotto is almost done, start the shrimp. Heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Cook the shrimp until pink, curled, and cooked through. Remove the shrimp with a slotted spoon to a plate. Add the wine and boil until thickened to about 2 tablespoons. Put the shrimp back into the pan, stirring to coat. Stir in the parsley, basil, salt, and pepper.
6. Spoon the shrimp over the risotto and serve.
Note: The Cibo brand of sun-dried tomato pesto used in this recipe can be found in the refrigerator section of major supermarkets.
